body:
This PR addresses issue #5316 to ensure that Poisson eigenvalues are `Float32` on Metal GPUs - right now FFTs don't work on Metal but when they are supported this will be necessary. To do this we pass the grid to `poisson_eigenvalues` - this would allow potential specification of grid float type for eigenvalues in the future (currently always using `Float64`, see [#55](https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/55)).

- Updates `poisson_eigenvalues` to take the `grid` as an argument
- Passes grid to `poisson_eigenvalues` wherever it's used
- Extends `poisson_eigenvalues` in `OceananigansMetalExt` (dispatched on `grid`) to output `Float32`
- Adds a test to `test_metal.jl`
title: Pass grid to poisson_eigenvalues and set eigenvalues to be `eltype(grid)`

body:
## Summary
- Adds `test/test_reactant_single_column_models.jl` with tests for `(Flat, Flat, Bounded)` single column grids on `ReactantState`
- Tests construction, compiled `time_step!`, `BuoyancyTracer`, `TKEDissipationVerticalDiffusivity`, and Enzyme reverse-mode gradients
- Includes the new test file in the `reactant_2` test group

## Motivation
Single column grids take a different code path through `update_state!` (via `single_column_model_mode.jl`) and `fill_halo_regions!` that is currently untested with Reactant. PR #5013 (single column state estimation example) uncovered that these code paths fail with `ReactantState` architecture. These tests will help identify and track the fixes needed.

## Expected failures
These tests are expected to fail initially — they expose missing Reactant support for single column grids. Known issues:
1. `ka_with_reactant` missing method for `fill_halo_regions!` on single column grids
2. `convert(Float64, TracedRNumber{Float64})` in `hydrostatic_ab2_step!`

## Test plan
- [ ] CI runs the `reactant_2` test group and reports failures
- [ ] Use failures to guide fixes for single column Reactant support

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation
labels.name: testing 🧪

title: Add Reactant tests for single column grids

body:
## Summary

- Fix method ambiguity error when using `ContinuousBoundaryFunction` (continuous-form `FluxBoundaryCondition`) on grids with Flat dimensions (e.g., `(Flat, Flat, Bounded)` for single-column models)
- The root cause: Flat dimensions have location `Nothing`, which collides with the convention that the boundary-normal dimension also has location `Nothing`, causing Julia to see multiple equally-specific `getbc` methods
- Add grid-type-constrained disambiguation methods for all combinations of two/three `Nothing` location parameters, for both domain boundary (2-index) and immersed boundary (3-index) `getbc` methods
- Add tests for `ContinuousBoundaryFunction` on all single-bounded-dimension Flat topologies

Reported by @ewquon

## Test plan

- [x] Verified fix on `(Flat, Flat, Bounded)`, `(Flat, Bounded, Flat)`, and `(Bounded, Flat, Flat)` topologies
- [ ] CI passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Fix ContinuousBoundaryFunction dispatch ambiguity on Flat grids
